pre_existing_findings.relevance: DIRECT — this lane addresses the root cause

pre_existing_findings.summary: ToolCache.inflight map mutated via direct reference mutation bypassing Ref.update

q1_tool_classification.description: All tool definition files classified as READ_ONLY (cacheable) or MUTATION (not cacheable)

READ_ONLY_cacheable.note: File glob pattern matching via ripgrep
Reads file contents with optional LSP context
Read source file digest: imports, exports, symbols
DuckDB queries against JSONL evidence artifacts
Validate JSON doc against JSON Schema

READ_ONLY_not_cacheable_yet.note: Network-dependent; could be cached with TTL

INFRASTRUCTURE.role: Bus event publishing for tool lifecycle
Converts Tool.Def to AI SDK JSON Schema

user_facing_tools_in_dot_opencode.note: Source file digest with artifactLog function

tool_def_cacheable_field.definition: cacheable?: boolean | { ttl?: Duration.Input; maxSize?: number }
tool_def_cacheable_field.current_usage: Used ONLY for semaphore routing in session/tools.ts:92 — cacheable tools get readSemaphore(10), non-cacheable get writeSemaphore(3). The { ttl, maxSize } object form is defined but NEVER instantiated.
tool_def_cacheable_field.span_annotation: tool.ts:241 sets 'tool.cacheable' span attribute as String(toolInfo.cacheable !== undefined)

capabilities: In-memory LRU cache with TTL-based expiry (default 5min)
Max entries cap (default 1000) with LRU eviction
Max entry size cap (default 1MB) — oversized results skip cache
Atomic reads via Ref.modify (no TOCTOU races)
Stats tracking: hits, misses, evictions, estimatedBytes
tool_cache_service.deprecated_comment: cache.ts:30 — '@deprecated Not currently wired to any consumer. Kept for future tool-level caching use. Remove if still unused after next audit.'
importers: packages/opencode/src/tool/tool.ts:8 — import * as ToolCache from './cache' (UNUSED in file)
packages/opencode/src/tool/registry.ts:189 — const cache = yield* ToolCache.Service (YIELDED but getOrCompute NEVER called)

artifactLog_function.signature: function artifactLog(context: any, event: Record<string, unknown>)
artifactLog_function.caching_behavior: NOT caching — this is an append-only activity log, not a read cache
artifactLog_function.relevance: Shows the docs/json/opencode/sessions/ pattern used for session-scoped JSONL artifacts. This pattern can be used for persistent cache storage.

registry_cache_state.details: State type includes cacheTTL: Duration.Duration (default Duration.minutes(30)) and cacheMaxSize: number. These are read from Config but NEVER forwarded to ToolCache.getOrCompute. Dead configuration.

q3_cache_disk_structure.rationale: Follows existing docs/json/opencode/ pattern used by sessions, plans, approvals, registry. The <tool_id>/<sha256_key> hierarchy keeps reads fast (no directory listing needed to find a key). JSONL index enables analytical queries via DuckDB (consistent with rig-jsonl-query pattern).

q4_cache_key_strategy.current_cache_ts: Opaque string keys — caller provides the key. No built-in serialization or argument hashing.

rationale.sha256: Already used extensively in codebase for content-addressing: review manifests (review/manifest.ts:17), findings dedup (out-of-scope-finding.ts, publish-finding.ts), feedback dedup (tool-feedback.ts), context packets (context/packet.ts). Consistent with existing patterns.
rationale.canonical_json: Parameter serialization must be deterministic. JSON.stringify with sorted keys ensures same logical args produce same key.
rationale.file_mtimes: Required for read tools (glob, grep, read, read-source) because file contents change. Including mtime of all files in the workspace snapshot makes cache auto-invalidate when files change. Tool defines which file paths it depends on — mtimes are read from InstanceState or AppFileSystem.
rationale.tool_id_prefix: Prevents key collisions between tools that use identical args (e.g., read filePath='foo' vs grep pattern='foo').

q4_cache_key_strategy.non_file_tools: For tools like rig-schema-validate which operate on pure string inputs (document, schema), only tool_id + serialized_params are needed — no file mtime. Key = SHA256(canonical_json([tool_id, params]))
q4_cache_key_strategy.example_key: 7f83b1657ff3c7f4e1e1e47e94d13c9e3e7f5d5d3f3c2e1a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8c for glob({pattern:'*.ts', path:'src'}) with workspace mtimes

current_mechanism.field: cacheable?: boolean | { ttl?: Duration.Input; maxSize?: number }
current_mechanism.set_on: Only `cacheable: true` used by 8 tools. The object form `{ ttl, maxSize }` is defined but never instantiated anywhere.

q5_cacheability_declaration.path_forward: Leverage existing cacheable field. When cacheable is true, use defaults (TTL 5min, maxSize 1MB). When cacheable is { ttl: '10 minutes', maxSize: 512KB }, override defaults. No new field needed.
q5_cacheability_declaration.per_tool_overrides: read-source could use longer TTL (30min) since source files change infrequently. greptools could use shorter TTL (2min) since they're fast to re-execute.

existing_in_codebase.pattern: In-memory TTL via Clock.currentTimeMillis + Duration. expiresAt = t + ttlMs. Expired entries evicted atomically on access. LRU eviction via Map insertion-order (splice-and-reinsert on hit). Max entries cap (1000) enforced before insertion. Max entry size cap (1MB).
Schedule.spaced(Duration.hours(1)) — periodic cleanup of stale truncation output files
Effect.cachedInvalidateWithTTL — caches global config load with TTL-based invalidation
lruSet() — manual LRU eviction for LLM model instances. Array-based order tracking with shift() for oldest eviction

q6_ttl_eviction_patterns.disk_persistence_ttl: For disk cache, TTL is enforced on read: check file mtime against cache entry expiry before returning. Stale files are deleted lazily on access (like cache.ts's expired eviction). A periodic cleanup Effect.forkDaemon can purge expired entries every hour (like truncate.ts pattern).
q6_ttl_eviction_patterns.eviction_on_write: When a mutation tool modifies a file, all cache entries keyed by that file's path should be invalidated. This requires a reverse index: file_path → set of cache keys. The registry.ts or session/tools.ts 'tool.execute.after' hook can trigger invalidation for write-side tools.

files_to_modify.changes: Wire ToolCache.getOrCompute into the wrap() execution pipeline. After decoding args but before calling execute(), if toolInfo.cacheable is truthy, call cache.getOrCompute(cacheKey, () => execute(args, ctx), cacheConfig). The cacheKey is derived from SHA256 of canonical JSON of [id, decodedArgs, fileMtimes].
files_to_modify.impact: LOW — additive change inside existing Effect.gen pipeline

files_to_modify.changes: 1. Wire cacheTTL and cacheMaxSize from State to actual ToolCache usage. 2. Build file_mtime index from InstanceState for cache key derivation. 3. Wire write-side invalidation: when mutation tools finish, invalidate cache entries for modified files. 4. Remove the dead `const cache = yield* ToolCache.Service` (line 189) and replace with actual usage.
files_to_modify.impact: MEDIUM — changes how tool cache is provisioned and invalidated

files_to_modify.changes: 1. Remove @deprecated annotation on getOrCompute (line 30). 2. Add new method: getOrComputeFile(path, key, compute, config) that also tracks file_path → cache_key mapping for write-side invalidation. 3. Add invalidateFile(path) method. 4. Optionally add disk persistence layer (write cache entries to filesystem, read on startup).
files_to_modify.impact: MEDIUM — extends existing service with new methods

files_to_modify.changes: In the 'tool.execute.after' plugin trigger (line 122), for mutation tools, call cache.invalidateFile(filePath) for each modified file to bust cached read results. This ensures that after a write/edit, subsequent reads don't return stale cache.

(if disk persistence) Add Effect layer for filesystem reads/writes via AppFileSystem. On startup, scan docs/json/opencode/cache/ for existing entries and rebuild in-memory index. On cache set, write entry to filesystem atomically.

new_files_to_create.purpose: Cache key derivation: SHA256(canonical_json([tool_id, params, file_mtimes])). Exports: deriveCacheKey(toolId, args, files). Canonical JSON serializer with sorted keys. File mtime reader from InstanceState/AppFileSystem.
new_files_to_create.rationale: Key derivation is complex enough to warrant its own module. Keeps cache.ts focused on storage logic.

new_files_to_create.purpose: Write-side cache invalidation: maintain file_path → set<CacheKey> reverse index. On mutation tool completion, invalidate all cache entries for touched files. Exports: trackFile(key, paths), invalidateFile(path).
new_files_to_create.rationale: Invalidation logic is separate from the core cache's hit/miss path.

new_files_to_create.purpose: Integration tests: read tool result cached on first call, returned on second call without re-execution. After edit tool modifies file, next read returns fresh (uncached) result. Multiple concurrent reads to same file coalesce into one execution.
new_files_to_create.rationale: Existing cache.test.ts tests cache unit behavior only. Need integration tests proving the cache is wired through the tool execution pipeline.

q8_dirty_files.opencode_tools_directory: CLEAN — no modified, staged, or untracked files in .opencode/tools/
q8_dirty_files.packages_opencode_src_tool_directory: CLEAN — no modified, staged, or untracked files in packages/opencode/src/tool/
q8_dirty_files.conflict_assessment: NONE — all dirty files are outside the tool/ directory. Zero contention with other lanes. Safe to proceed.

smoking_guns.title: ToolCache is fully built but dead code
smoking_guns.detail: cache.ts implements LRU, TTL, inflight dedup, stats, eviction — all production quality. But getOrCompute is marked @deprecated (line 30) with comment 'Not currently wired to any consumer.' The Service is provided in AppLayer (app-runtime.ts:105) and yielded in registry.ts:189 but its methods are NEVER called anywhere.
smoking_guns.action: Wire getOrCompute into tool/tool.ts::wrap() to make the cache actually serve tool results.

smoking_guns.title: cacheable field exists but only controls semaphore routing
smoking_guns.detail: Tool.Def has cacheable?: boolean | { ttl, maxSize } (tool.ts:104). 8 tools set cacheable: true. The ONLY runtime effect is in session/tools.ts:92 where cacheable tools get readSemaphore(10). No results are actually cached. The { ttl, maxSize } object form has zero consumers.
smoking_guns.action: Use the existing cacheable field to drive actual caching behavior. The type is already right — just needs implementation.

smoking_guns.title: No cache key derivation strategy
smoking_guns.detail: cache.ts uses opaque string keys (type CacheKey = string). No built-in serialization of tool args or file state into a content-addressable key. SHA256 content-addressing exists elsewhere in the codebase (review manifests, findings) but not for tool results.
smoking_guns.action: Create cache-key.ts with SHA256(canonical_json([tool_id, params, file_mtimes])). Import pattern from existing content-addressing in review/manifest.ts and tool/out-of-scope-finding.ts.

smoking_guns.title: No write-side cache invalidation
smoking_guns.detail: When edit/write/smart-edit/smart-batch modify files, there is no mechanism to invalidate cached read/glob/grep/read-source results. The cache would serve stale data indefinitely (until TTL expiry).
smoking_guns.action: Add cache-invalidation.ts with file_path → cache_key reverse index. Wire into session/tools.ts 'tool.execute.after' plugin hook to invalidate on write.

smoking_guns.title: Cache is memory-only — no cross-session reuse
smoking_guns.detail: All cache entries live in Ref<Map> — lost on process restart. The docs/json/opencode/ pattern already exists for persistent artifacts (sessions, plans, analytics). Disk persistence would enable cross-session cache reuse.
smoking_guns.action: Phase 2: Add disk persistence layer. Cache entries written to docs/json/opencode/cache/tool-results/<tool_id>/<sha256>.json. Index rebuilt on startup.

smoking_guns.title: User-facing .opencode/tools/ has no cacheable concept
smoking_guns.detail: The 8 user-facing tools in .opencode/tools/ use @opencode-ai/plugin tool() factory — a different API surface from Tool.define(). These tools have no cacheable field and no access to ToolCache service.
smoking_guns.action: Phase 3 (future): Expose cacheable flag in plugin tool() API. Plugin tools that declare cacheable: true participate in the same ToolCache as built-in tools.

phase_1_core_wiring.description: Wire existing ToolCache into the tool execution pipeline. Minimum viable caching.
deliverables: Cache key derivation (SHA256 of tool_id + args + file mtimes)
ToolCache.getOrCompute called from tool.ts::wrap() for cacheable tools
Tests: cache hit/miss for read tool, concurrent coalescing

phase_2_invalidation.description: Write-side cache invalidation so mutation tools bust stale cache.
deliverables: Invalidation on mutation tool completion via 'tool.execute.after' hook
Tests: edit file → next read returns fresh result

phase_3_disk_persistence.description: Persist cache to disk for cross-session reuse.
deliverables: Rebuild index on startup from disk

phase_4_plugin_tools.description: Expose cacheable flag to user-facing .opencode/tools/ via plugin API.
deliverables: cacheable field on plugin tool() def

key_edges: tool/tool.ts → tool/cache-key.ts (derive key from args + state)
dependency_graph_summary.service_graph: All three tool services are co-equal peers in AppLayer (Layer.mergeAll). No nesting, no hidden provisioning.
